notes: Source primary key. decimal(10,0) carries no fractional digits; INT64 is the correct BigQuery integer type. Pre-load uniqueness check required; post-load PK uniqueness assertion must pass before load is marked complete.
NOT NULL in source. BigQuery STRING has no length constraint; original 50-char ceiling is not enforced in target but should be flagged in schema-compatibility check if source length changes.
NOT NULL in source. Same length-constraint consideration as first_name.
NOT NULL in source. Null-constraint violation must be logged and reported before the load is marked complete per acceptance criteria.
Nullable in source. NULLs must be preserved in BigQuery. Stored as STRING to accommodate formatted phone strings (dashes, parentheses, country codes).
NOT NULL in source. Direct DATE-to-DATE mapping; MySQL DATE and BigQuery DATE are both calendar-date types with no time or timezone component. No conversion risk.
Cluster column 2 of 3. NOT NULL in source. Moderate-cardinality code field used frequently in job-level analytics filters; second position in cluster key after department_id.

NOT NULL in source. BigQuery NUMERIC (up to 29 integer digits, 9 fractional digits) safely covers decimal(8,2). Preserves exact decimal arithmetic for financial aggregations; FLOAT64 is deliberately avoided to prevent rounding errors.
Nullable. NULL signifies the employee has no commission plan. NUMERIC preserves exact decimal(4,2) precision. Null-constraint report must document NULL count before load completion sign-off.

Nullable self-referential FK; NULL indicates no manager (top of org hierarchy). decimal(10,0) mapped to INT64 consistent with employee_id. Referential integrity is not enforced natively in BigQuery; validate via post-load assertion if required.

Cluster column 1 of 3 (leading cluster key). Nullable. decimal(4,0) mapped to INT64. Department grouping is the most common high-level filter in analytics queries; placing it first in the cluster key maximises partition pruning benefit.

Cluster column 3 of 3. NOT NULL. Low-cardinality field (e.g. ACTIVE / INACTIVE / TERMINATED). Placed last in cluster key because low cardinality is most effective as a trailing filter after higher-cardinality leading keys.

Partition column — DAY granularity. NOT NULL; chosen over updated_date as partition key because it is non-nullable, ensuring every row lands in a deterministic partition. MySQL TIMESTAMP is stored in UTC; BigQuery TIMESTAMP is also UTC — no timezone conversion required. Drives time-range query pruning and incremental load window scoping.

Nullable. Primary delta-tracking watermark for incremental loads: pipeline records MAX(updated_date) from the previous run and queries MySQL WHERE UPDATED_DATE > {watermark} to capture changed rows. NULL on rows that have never been updated after initial insert. MySQL TIMESTAMP is UTC; BigQuery TIMESTAMP is UTC — no conversion required.
